pages.title: College of Arts & Letters | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Arts & Letters comprises 10 innovative departments focused on real-world partnerships, transformational experiences and career-tailored programs.
headings: Bold Ideas, Real Impact, Lifelong Belonging
"Welcome to A&L! Where our students graduate ready to lead —not just succeed.
For Our Current Students — Leading From Where You Are
For Our Future Hornets — Your Journey Starts Here
For Our Alumni — Your Legacy, Our Inspiration
Come As You Are, Grow As You Dream
Pillars of Distinction: Anchoring Your Journey in Arts & Letters
Advancing lives through innovation and transformative education
Sac State Arts & Letters: Leading. Impactful. Certified for Excellence
College of Arts and Letters Calendar
contentBlocks: We are a destination for thinkers, doers, artists, advocates, innovators, and entrepreneurs. Our students, scholars, and communities come together to shape a more just, dynamic, and interconnected world."

You are the heart of Arts & Letters. Every class you take, every project you lead, and every voice you raise shapes the culture of our College. Here, you’re not just preparing for the future — you’re creating it, one bold idea and one meaningful action at a time.

If you’re ready to think critically, create fearlessly, and lead with purpose, this is where you belong. At Arts & Letters, you’ll find mentors who believe in you, opportunities that challenge you, and a community that will champion your growth from day one.

Your achievements light the path for the next generation. Whether you’re shaping industries, advancing the arts, or driving change in your community, you remain an essential part of our story — and a living example of what’s possible with an Arts & Letters education.

Watch a powerful short film featuring Dean Gladys M. Francis, Ashley Johnson (a graduate student) and Dr. Michele Foss-Snowden (her former professor and mentor). Together, they share stories of transformation, resilience, and the lifelong impact of mentorship — proof that changemaking begins with connection.

Arts & Letters is a community where your voice, culture, and creativity shape everything we do. We bring together students from all walks of life to learn, create, and grow—side by side. Through shared stories, bold ideas, and deep collaboration, we build a space where everyone belongs and brilliance thrives. Here, your journey is honored, and your future is imagined with purpose.

Care is our foundation. Faculty and staff create a vibrant, inclusive community where students are supported, challenged, and inspired. Our faculty mentor with compassion and bring boundary-breaking research into the classroom. Our staff nurture belonging, sustain our operations, and ensure every student feels seen. Together, we cultivate connection, purpose, and possibility.

The College of Arts & Letters at Sacramento State fosters academic excellence, inclusive leadership, and career readiness. With global engagement, community partnerships, and transformative teaching, we champion social mobility and local impact with global reach. Our students graduate ready to lead, creating meaningful change across industries, communities, and cultures.

Our faculty are award-winning educators and global experts who mentor with rigor and heart. Students gain hands-on learning and interdisciplinary skills to lead across sectors. Arts & Letters grads excel in strategic communication, design thinking, and cultural agility. Sacramento is our living lab; the world, our classroom. We shape national discourse through acclaimed research, creative work, and civic collaboration.
Thurs, Feb 12 - Thurs, Apr 30 Painting La Comuniversidad
Thurs, Feb 12 - Thurs, Apr 30 Three Murals of the Royal Chicano Air Force
Sun, Apr 26 SOLD OUT - Music Under the Dome
Sun, Apr 26 World Music: South Indian Classical

pages.title: You Are the Heart of Arts & Letters | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information for Current Students
headings: Current Students Leading From Where You Are
You Are the Heart of Arts & Letters
Our College is not defined by buildings or programs — it is defined by you. Every class you take, every project you lead, and every voice you raise shapes the culture of Arts & Letters.
contentBlocks: From academic advising and scholarships to wellness support, we are committed to ensuring you have the tools you need to succeed.
Join Us! Apply Now! Visit Our Admissions Webpage

Leadership is not a title — it’s a practice. Whether you’re running a student organization, mentoring peers, or curating an exhibit, you are already leading from where you are.
Discover Clubs, Organizations & Activities

Our calendar is alive with performances, lectures, exhibits, and festivals. These events are more than entertainment — they are opportunities to connect, to learn, and to celebrate the richness of our community.
We shine a spotlight on student achievements because your success is our story. Every award, every performance, every breakthrough matters. It is your A&L journey !

pages.title: Think Critically. Create Fearlessly. Lead with Purpose. | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information for prospective students
headings: Your Journey Starts Here Welcome to Arts & Letters
Think Critically. Create Fearlessly. Lead with Purpose.

Choosing a college is choosing a community. At Sacramento State’s College of Arts & Letters, you’ll find mentors who believe in you, opportunities that challenge you, and a community that will champion your growth from day one. Whether you're an artist, advocate, storyteller, or scholar — this is where your journey begins.

We are a destination for thinkers, doers, artists, and changemakers. Your voice matters. Your culture is celebrated. Your dreams are nurtured. Here, you’ll be empowered to explore your passions, challenge assumptions, and make a meaningful impact.
contentBlocks: With majors and minors across the arts, humanities, and social sciences, you can design a path that reflects your interests and prepares you for your future.
Explore disciplines like Art, Communication Studies, Design, English, History, Humanities and Religious Studies, Music, Philosophy, Theatre and Dance, World Languages and Literatures, and more.
Combine fields to create interdisciplinary experiences that match your goals.
Work closely with faculty who are leaders in their fields — and invested in your success.

Belonging is at the heart of Arts & Letters! Join student organizations that reflect your identity, interests, and ambitions. Celebrate culture through festivals, performances, and community events . Find your people — and build lifelong connections within Arts & Letters.
Ready to begin? Explore admissions requirements and deadlines. Learn about scholarships and financial aid. Schedule a campus tour and experience Arts & Letters in action.
Find Additional University Funding Support

links.text: Think Critically. Create Fearlessly. Lead with Purpose.
Begin Your Student Journey in A&L

pages.title: Innovation in Action | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page is dedicated to innovation in the College of Arts and Letters
headings: Innovation at Arts & Letters Where Ideas Take Flight

At Sacramento State’s College of Arts & Letters, innovation is more than a buzzword —it’s the way we teach, create, and collaborate. Our faculty and students push boundaries across disciplines, blending research, creativity, and technology to address the challenges of today and shape the ideas of tomorrow. Innovation in Arts & Letters is about more than invention — it’s about transforming ideas into action . Whether through groundbreaking research, creative exploration, or collaborative experimentation, our college is a hub where imagination meets momentum. Your journey starts here !

From communication studies to digital media, our scholarship drives new knowledge and solutions that resonate locally and globally. Artists, thinkers, writers, and performers reimagine traditions and pioneer new forms of expression, expanding the possibilities of human imagination.
Alumni Impact: Leading Beyond the Classroom
Sacramento is our canvas. Students engage with the city through internships, performances, exhibitions, and community projects — turning theory into practice and classrooms into catalysts.

We connect with industry, community, and international partners to expand opportunities for our students and amplify their work. These collaborations fuel innovation, open doors, and ensure our work resonates far beyond campus.

Students gain the skills to thrive in evolving fields — critical thinking, adaptability, and creative problem-solving that prepare them for what’s next. We prepare students for industries not yet imagined. With skills in design thinking, communication, and cultural agility, our graduates are ready to lead in a rapidly changing world. We don’t prepare students for jobs — we prepare them for futures that don’t exist yet.

In the College of Arts & Letters, future-readiness means cultivating minds that can adapt, invent, and lead across disciplines, industries, and cultures. Our graduates are not just employable — they’re indispensable.
contentBlocks: Our faculty are nationally and internationally recognized scholars and artists whose work shapes conversations in academia, policy, and culture. They bring this cutting-edge research into the classroom, ensuring students learn from leaders at the forefront of discovery.
They publish in leading university presses and journals, exhibit in major galleries, and present at global conferences — advancing knowledge across disciplines.
Their work often bridges theory and practice, influencing public discourse, cultural institutions, and emerging industries.

Students are not just consumers of knowledge — they are creators. From digital storytelling and design thinking to entrepreneurial ventures and public scholarship, our students are building the future now.

They publish in peer reviewed journals. They launch podcasts, zines, and media platforms that amplify underrepresented voices and challenge dominant narratives. They win national awards for their research and community service.

They collaborate across departments to build immersive experiences —from augmented reality exhibits to civic engagement campaigns. They prototype solutions to real-world problems, applying creative thinking to social, environmental, and cultural challenges.

Our alumni carry the spirit of Arts & Letters into the world — as changemakers, creators, and thought leaders. They apply their education to industries, communities, and causes across the globe, proving that boundary-breaking creativity doesn’t end at graduation — it evolves.
Faculty lead bold initiatives that connect research with real-world impact — from public humanities projects to immersive media installations.
Students contribute fresh perspectives, applying their creativity and curiosity to civic challenges, cultural programming, and entrepreneurial ventures.
Community partners co-create solutions, offering lived expertise, mentorship, and platforms for engagement that extend far beyond campus.
Faculty and students collaborate with city agencies, nonprofits, and arts organizations to design public-facing projects.
Courses become launchpads for community-engaged research, multilingual storytelling, and cultural revitalization.
Public performances, exhibitions, and digital campaigns bring academic work into the streets, amplifying its reach and relevance.
Students collaborate with local arts organizations to produce public exhibitions and performances.
They contribute to civic initiatives through storytelling, media production, and multilingual outreach.
Faculty-led projects connect students with city agencies, nonprofits, and cultural institutions for hands-on learning.
Students intern with local museums, theaters, and media outlets to gain real-world experience.
They design and implement community-based research projects that address regional challenges.
They showcase their work in public forums — from pop-up galleries to citywide festivals — amplifying their impact beyond campus.
Creative industries that offer internships, mentorships, and real-world platforms for student work.
Community organizations that co-design projects rooted in cultural relevance and civic engagement.
International institutions that foster cross-cultural exchange, research partnerships, and global learning experiences.
Faculty co-author research with global scholars and present at international conferences.
Students participate in study abroad programs, virtual exchanges, and global media projects.
Alumni and partners return as guest speakers, project mentors, and collaborators — creating a cycle of innovation and impact. Explore Alumni Stories .
Faculty design learning environments that mirror the complexity of the world — interdisciplinary, collaborative, and constantly evolving.
Students learn to navigate ambiguity , ask better questions, and build solutions that are as imaginative as they are practical.
Courses integrate design thinking, cultural fluency, and ethical leadership , preparing students to shape the future, not just survive it.
Students prototype ideas in real time — from immersive media to multilingual campaigns — and test them in public.
They graduate with portfolios that reflect not just what they know, but how they think and what they can build.
They enter the world as agile creators, strategic communicators, and culturally attuned leaders — ready to thrive in industries not yet imagined.
Research with Impact: Boundary-Breaking Creativity

pages.title: Your Story, Your Path, Our Community | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information for students starting their academic journey
headings: Student Journey Come As You Are, Grow As You Dream
Your Story, Your Path, Our Community
Watch the Campus Master Plan Video (Forthcoming: A Mentorship Journey)
contentBlocks: Every student’s journey is unique, but all are supported by the A&L community. From your first day on campus to the moment you cross the graduation stage, we are with you every step of the way.

Forthcoming: Watch how Ashley found her voice — and her purpose — through a transformative relationship with her graduate mentor, Dr. Foss-Snowden. A story of growth, guidance, and the power of community. ---

While we await the inspiring story of one of our students and her mentor, we invite you to explore a visionary glimpse into the future of our campus. This video, part of Sacramento State’s Campus Master Plan, showcases the transformative developments designed to support student success—from cutting-edge science and engineering buildings to vibrant arts and wellness spaces.

Transitioning to college is both thrilling and overwhelming — and we’re here to help you navigate it. Our orientation programs are designed to welcome you into the Arts & Letters family, while peer mentors and dedicated advisors ensure you feel supported from day one. You’ll build confidence , find your rhythm, and begin to see yourself not just as a student, but as a creator of your own journey.
Transitioning to college is exciting — and sometimes overwhelming.
Our orientation programs help you feel at home from day one.
Peer mentors and advisors guide you through your first year.
You’ll build confidence, community, and a sense of belonging.

College is more than a major — it’s a time to discover who you are and what you stand for. At Arts & Letters, we encourage exploration across disciplines, allowing you to follow your curiosity and connect ideas in unexpected ways. Whether you’re diving into philosophy, media, design, or history, you’ll build intellectual agility and creative resilience that will serve you for life.
College is a time to discover who you are and what you love.
Explore courses across the arts, humanities, and social sciences.
Try new disciplines, challenge assumptions, and follow your curiosity.
Build skills that serve you for life — not just for a job.

You are never alone in your journey. Our faculty are more than instructors — they are mentors, collaborators, and champions of your growth. Peers become creative partners and lifelong friends, and together you’ll find spaces that honor your identity, culture, and dreams. Belonging here means being seen, heard, and supported — every step of the way.
Faculty walk alongside you as mentors, collaborators, and champions.
Peers become creative partners and lifelong friends.
You’ll find spaces that honor your identity, culture, and dreams.

Graduation is not the end — it’s a milestone in a lifelong journey. As you step into the world, you’ll carry with you the knowledge, skills, and spirit of Arts & Letters. Our alumni lead in industries, communities, and creative fields around the globe, and they continue to embody the values of critical thinking, fearless creativity, and purposeful leadership. Wherever you go next, you’ll take us with you.
Graduation is not the end — it’s a milestone.
You’ll leave with more than a degree: you’ll carry the spirit of Arts & Letters.
Our alumni lead in industries, communities, and creative fields around the world.
Wherever you go next, you’ll take us with you.
Watch the Campus Master Plan Video (Forthcoming: A Mentorship Journey)

pages.title: Ways To Give | Sacramento State
pages.description: Learn how you can partner with the College of Arts & Letters to elevate our impact for the betterment of our region, state, and world.
headings: The College of Arts & Letters is On The Rise!
contentBlocks: Located in the capital city of one of the most economically and culturally influential states in the world, Sacramento State’s College of Arts & Letters is uniquely positioned to prepare and empower students to become the creative leaders, artists, performers, educators, communicators, and critical thinkers our society needs for advancing the human condition. This College is a proud part of the region’s growing creative economy and is a driving force in the region’s cultural renaissance.
Alumna Elaine Welteroth is currently a host on the Daytime Emmy-award winning talk show "The Talk". She shares how Sacramento State has made a lasting impact on her career and life.

We invite you to partner with us and elevate our impact for the betterment of our region, state, and world. Contact us to learn more about our fundraising priorities or you would like to discover additional ways to get involved.
Tess Dyer, '05, MA '15 Director of Development tdyer@csus.edu (916) 278-5184

links.text: Sacramento State has made a lasting impact

pages.title: Call for Board Members! | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Arts and Letters is launching two transformative boards to help realize its bold vision and shape the future of liberal arts education.
headings: Call for Board Members! Help Make A&L a Destination College!

The College of Arts and Letters (A&L) is launching two transformative boards to help realize its bold vision: Make A&L a Destination College and a Model in Modernizing the Academy. We invite visionary leaders, alumni, scholars, creative, and industry professionals to join us in shaping the future of liberal arts education.
A&L Executive Brochure: Vision in Action
contentBlocks: The A&L Advisory Board guides the College's strategic direction, affirm its academic excellence, and strengthen its local, national, and international reputation as a hub for transformative research, interdisciplinary collaboration, and inclusive education.
Scholars and thought leaders across the humanities, arts, and social sciences
Advise on A&L’s strategic initiatives and academic priorities
Support faculty development, student success, and curriculum innovation
Champion A&L’s DEIAB efforts and global engagement
Help seed new centers, fellowships, and signature programs
Serve as ambassadors for A&L’s mission and impact

The A&L Industry & Innovation Council serves as a dynamic engine for innovation, collaboration, and transformation across the College. This council connects academic excellence with real-world impact—mobilizing industry expertise, funding, and strategic partnerships to modernize liberal arts education and empower the next generation of global leaders.
Executives in media, tech, healthcare, publishing, education, and public service
Entrepreneurs, startup founders, and innovation strategists
Leaders in AI, data ethics, and digital transformation
HR and talent development professionals focused on future-ready skills
Alumni working in interdisciplinary, global, and emerging fields
Cultural, nonprofit, and civic leaders advancing equity and access
Creatives and communicators shaping the future of storytelling, design, and performance
Business and leadership experts driving organizational change and impact
Facilitate funding through philanthropic gifts, grants, and strategic investments
Co-create internships, externships, co-ops, and fellowships across industries
Advise on stackable credentials, certificates, and joint degrees
Align A&L programs with emerging market needs and interdisciplinary trends
Offer career coaching, leadership development, and real-world insight
Champion A&L’s mission, DEIAB efforts, and global reach
Influence the future of higher education and liberal arts
Empower diverse students to thrive in a rapidly evolving world
Collaborate with visionary faculty and leaders across disciplines
Shape programs that integrate creativity, technology, and social impact
Build a legacy of innovation, equity, and excellence in education
Join a vibrant network of scholars, creatives, and industry pioneers

Want to learn more about our strategic direction, leadership priorities, and transformative goals? Download the College of Arts & Letters Executive Brochure to explore how we’re building a Destination College and modernizing the academy through creativity, equity, and collaboration.
Access to Our A&L Executive Brochure A roadmap for innovation, impact, and inclusive excellence.
Let’s build the future of the academy—together!
We are currently accepting nominations and expressions of interest for both boards. To apply or nominate a candidate, please submit:
Indication of which board you’d like to join and your preferred area of engagement (e.g., funding, mentorship, curriculum, internships)

pages.title: Meet Dr. Gladys M. FRANCIS | Sacramento State
pages.description: Meet Dr. Gladys M. FRANCIS
headings: Dr. Gladys M. Francis Dean, College of Arts & Letters
Transforming Higher Education Through Innovation, Equity, and Global Engagement
Shaping the Future of Arts & Letters
Creating a culture of belonging that fuels academic excellence
Positioning our College as a leader
Advancing Career Readiness Through Innovation and Industry Collaboration
Purdue University, Department of Foreign Languages & Literatures - West Lafayette, IN
University of the Antilles – Schoelcher Martinique, France
Masters in Education/Second Language Acquisition Methodologies (Network Technology Integration in Education)
Bachelors in the Humanities (Licence de Lettres Modernes)
The Pulse of The College of A&L
contentBlocks: As Dean of the College of Arts & Letters at Sacramento State University,Dr. Gladys M. Francis draws on more than a decade of transformative leadership at major institutions to expand opportunities for our students, strengthen community partnerships, and elevate our national and global profile.

Dr. Gladys M. Francis is a Distinguished Professor of Africana, French, and Francophone Studies. She is a nationally and internationally recognized leader whose career bridges scholarship, innovation, and transformative academic leadership.
Recipient of over $10.4M in research funding
Raised $9M+ in funding and $500K in-kind to expand global, interdisciplinary education, and student opportunities
Raised $2.3M for curriculum‑to‑career initiatives
Directed 500+ international collaborative projects
Created 330+ internships , including placements in Africa and Europe
Founded the Engaging the World program, securing $1.1M for digital pedagogy innovation
Author of 40+ articles and 4 books , including her latest with Yale University Press

As Dean of the College of Arts & Letters at Sacramento State, I am committed to expanding opportunities for our students, strengthening our community partnerships, and elevating our national and global profile. My leadership is grounded in a proven record of transforming academic programs, securing resources, and building pathways that prepare students not just to succeed, but to lead.
I believe higher education must be bold, relevant, and globally connected—serving as both a local anchor and a catalyst for addressing 21st‑century challenges. At Sacramento State, this means:
By fostering a vibrant academic community where a wide range of perspectives, experiences, and talents are not only welcomed but actively shape teaching, research, and innovation

We are anhored in Sacramento’s civic, cultural, and creative life while forging global partnerships.Through strategic partnerships at home and abroad, we connect campus expertise to pressing global challenges—aiming to transform the College into both a trusted community leader and a catalyst for international collaboration.

By designing forward‑thinking curricula and forging strong ties with industry leaders, ensuring students graduate with the skills, adaptability, and professional networks to thrive in a rapidly evolving job market.

By implementing initiatives with built‑in longevity—programs, policies, and partnerships designed to be adaptable, scalable, and impactful for years to come, leaving a legacy of sustained growth and opportunity for the College of Arts and Letters.

Before joining Sacramento State, I led initiatives that connected campuses to the world—creating study abroad pipelines, building 130+ global partnerships, and designing programs that foster global citizenship. These experiences now inform our College’s expanding network of international opportunities.

I have developed and launched hundreds of internships—both paid and international—that placed students in roles across Africa, Europe, and the United States , giving them real‑world experience and professional networks before graduation.

At Georgia State University , I led a comprehensive overhaul of academic programs to embed digital literacy, leadership development, and experiential learning into the core curriculum. These reforms boosted graduate and undergraduate enrollment, reduced time‑to‑degree, and secured funding to sustain internships, practicum courses, and professional development opportunities that connected students directly to industry and community partners.

At Howard University , I scaled study abroad and experiential learning opportunities to reach all 4,300 students in the College of Arts and Sciences—leveraging Washington, D.C.’s unparalleled civic, cultural, and policy resources to prepare graduates for leadership across sectors (the inaugural pipeline extended study abroad opportunities to hundreds of students who travelled to France, Italy, Ghana, Greece, Guadeloupe, Kenya, Martinique, South Africa, etc.).

At Howard University, I also founded the Ambassador Leadership Program , a $250,000‑funded initiative that expanded students’ access to high‑impact leadership training, mentorship, and global engagement experiences (e.g., England, France, Tobago). This program not only cultivated student leaders on campus but also positioned them as visible representatives of the university in national and international arenas.

I have secured over $1.1M for digital pedagogy initiatives, founded the Engaging the World program to promote technology‑enhanced learning, and directed hundreds of collaborative projects that brought together academia, industry, government, and the arts. These efforts have reached more than 24,000 participants worldwide.

As a Distinguished Professor of Africana, French, and Francophone Studies, my research explores identity, representation, and social justice across cultures and media. I have published 40+ articles, three books, and have a fourth forthcoming with Yale University Press in 2025. My work has been featured in international conferences, keynote addresses, and collaborative publications with students.
Literatures and Visual Arts (Caribbean, Maghreb, Sub-Saharan Africa)
Digital Pedagogy and Instructional Technology
Masters in French Studies and French Second Language Acquisition
Masters in Education/Second Language Acquisition Methodologies (Network Technology Integration in Education)

The true brilliance of Arts & Letters lives in its people. Our faculty—globally recognized artists, scholars, and thought leaders—bring innovation into every lecture hall and research space. Our faculty’s work transcends disciplines and borders. They bring cutting-edge research into the classroom, mentor students with compassion and rigor, and build programs that redefine what higher education can achieve.

Our staff—solutions-driven, and mission-focused—create the infrastructure of care that propels us forward. They are dedicated, visionary, and deeply student-centered. They make all of it possible. They sustain our operations, nurture our community, and ensure that every student who enters our college is met with encouragement and support.
Together, we are architects of opportunity and the reason this college thrives. Together, we create pathways for students to explore their passions, challenge convention, and find belonging.

Connect with Dean Gladys M. Francis on LinkedIn to follow her leadership journey, explore initiatives in the College of Arts & Letters, and engage with her work advancing innovation, student success, and global collaboration.
Connect/Engage With | Follow Dean Francis

pages.title: Art | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Art Department at Sacramento State fosters expression through visual arts via courses in art education, art history, ceramics, drawing, new media art, painting, printmaking, and interdisciplinary sculpture.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Engage With the World of Art
contentBlocks: The Department of Art introduces students to the expressive world of the visual arts. Students are able to explore art through courses in art education, art history, ceramics, drawing, new media art, painting, printmaking, sculpture, and social practice.

The Department’s undergraduate program offers courses in four general areas Art History, Studio Arts Methods, and Art Studio, while the fourth prepares students to teach with a Master of Arts degree in Studio Art .

pages.description: Alumni Page
headings: A&L Alumni Your Legacy, Our Inspiration
Once a Hornet, Always a Changemaker

Graduating from Arts & Letters is not the end of your story with us — it’s the beginning of a lifelong connection. Our alumni are innovators, advocates, and leaders who carry the spirit of Sac State into every corner of the world. Whether you are shaping industries, advancing the arts, or driving social change, you remain an essential part of our story.
Your gifts fund scholarships and fuel innovation!

Every alum carries a piece of Arts & Letters into the world. From muralists whose work transforms cityscapes, to journalists amplifying unheard voices, to educators shaping the next generation — your achievements remind us that an A&L education is not just about knowledge, but about courage, creativity, and impact.

The College of Arts & Letters is proud to announce that Shelley Wetton (B.A., M.A. English) has been named our 2025 Distinguished Alumni Award recipient. As Senior Vice President and Chief Marketing Officer at Five Star Bank, she has become a nationally respected leader in branding and strategic communications. Honored as Chief Marketing Officer of the Year by the Sacramento Business Journal in 2023, she continues to shape her industry while serving on multiple community boards. Her journey from an English graduate in our College to nationally recognized executive is a powerful reminder of what’s possible when talent, vision, and purpose come together. She embodies the excellence, creativity, and leadership that define our alumni community—and the transformative power of an Arts & Letters education.

We invite you to remain part of our vibrant community. Attend alumni reunions, mentor current students, or join advisory boards that shape the future of our College. Your presence strengthens the bridge between past and future Hornets.
Supporting Arts & Letters means investing in possibility. Your gifts fund scholarships, fuel innovation, and ensure that every student — regardless of background — has the opportunity to thrive.

Supporting Arts & Letters means investing in possibility — not just for one student, but for generations of changemakers who will carry their education into the world. Every contribution you make helps us open doors that might otherwise remain closed. Your generosity provides scholarships that ease financial burdens, creates access to transformative opportunities, and ensures that every student — regardless of background — has the chance to thrive.

But your impact extends far beyond financial support. Contributions to Arts & Letters fuel innovation in classrooms, studios, and performance halls. They allow faculty and students to collaborate on groundbreaking research, launch creative projects that inspire dialogue, and bring bold ideas to life in ways that ripple far beyond campus. With your support, we can continue to nurture the next generation of artists, advocates, educators, and leaders who will shape industries and communities with vision and compassion.

When you Give Back , you are not only honoring the legacy of Arts & Letters — you are actively building its future. Together, we can ensure that every Hornet has the resources, mentorship, and encouragement to imagine boldly, act courageously, and lead with purpose.
As a Hornet for life, you have access to career services, professional development, and a network of peers who share your passion for making a difference.

pages.title: History | Sacramento State
pages.description: Studying in the History Department at Sacramento State University, you acquire skills in critical thinking, research, and persuasion. These skills can be applied anywhere. History majors work as teachers, lawyers, in business, finance, healthcare, government, communications, non-profits, public service, and really, almost everywhere.
headings: Department of History College of Arts & Letters
"So, what are you going to do with a history major?"
contentBlocks: That's a fair question. For many, the entire point of pursuing a college education is to open opportunities for a better job or better pay — to do something, and what can you do by studying the past? Quite a lot, actually. History is about more than just knowing the past. It's about understanding why things happened as they did and how they continue to shape our world today.

History majors work as teachers, lawyers, in business, finance, healthcare, government, communications, non-profits, public service, and really, almost everywhere. By studying history, you will acquire skills that help you think critically about a subject, locate evidence, understand perspectives and biases, and make persuasive arguments. These skills can be applied anywhere. Indeed, these are precisely the skills most employers are looking for in prospective applicants. So perhaps the question should be, "why NOT study history?"
History BA (for students starting in Fall 2023)
History BA (Law & Social Justice Concentration)
History BA (for students starting before Fall 2023)
Recent History and Public History MA Graduates

We acknowledge the Nisenan people on whose unceded lands we are gathering and the Puhtwin and Miwok peoples whose unceded homelands also include the city of Sacramento. We also acknowledge the sovereign tribes of Nisenan, Wintun, Miwok, Me-Wuk, and Maidu peoples in the wider Sacramento region.

pages.title: English | Sacramento State
pages.description: The English Department at Sac State offers degrees at the undergraduate & graduate levels, Pre-Credential Preparation, & minors in Creative Writing & Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages.
headings: Department of English College of Arts & Letters
contentBlocks: The Department of English at Sacramento State is a distinguished community of educators, scholars, writers, and professional staff dedicated to the advancement of knowledge in literature, writing, linguistics, media, and cultural studies.

Designed to maintain academic rigor while allowing students to explore their interests, our curriculum blends traditional methods in language, literature, and writing studies with innovative approaches to media, technology, and creative expression. We offer two Bachelor of Arts degrees (English and English-Pre-credential) along with minors in English, Creative Writing, and Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages (TESOL). Students may also pursue certificates in TESOL, Professional Writing, Teaching Composition, and Writing for Film, Stage, and Television.

Through interdisciplinary collaboration, service-learning initiatives, and a commitment to inclusive education, the department prepares students for diverse career paths both within and beyond academia. As students engage in analytical study of texts, language, and culture, they develop critical thinking, cultural literacy, and imaginative insight--essential skills for contributing meaningfully to diverse communities.
Department Chair Office Hours Spring 2026: Tues/Wed 3:00-4:00PM (CLV 106)
Department of English Calaveras Hall, 104 Hours vary
Follow us on https://www.linkedin.com/in/english-department-sacramento-state-01b11937a/

pages.title: Ready to Lead, Ready to Thrive | Sacramento State
pages.description: Career Ready Page
headings: Career Ready From Classroom to Career
Ready to Lead, Ready to Thrive

At Arts & Letters, we don’t just prepare students for jobs — we prepare them for lives of purpose. Our graduates are storytellers, problem‑solvers, and visionaries who bring creativity and cultural agility into every field they enter.
contentBlocks: With an A&L degree, the possibilities are boundless. Our alumni are filmmakers, designers, policy makers, entrepreneurs, educators, and advocates. They lead in boardrooms, classrooms, studios, and communities.

Sacramento is our living laboratory. Students intern at museums, nonprofits, media outlets, and government agencies, applying their skills in real world contexts while building networks that last a lifetime.

We provide tailored support — from resume workshops and interview coaching to networking events with industry leaders. Our goal is simple: to ensure every student leaves Arts & Letters confident, connected, and career ready.
We want to celebrate your success and help future Hornets follow in your footsteps.
Connect with us on LinkedIn : Join the Sac State Arts & Letters LinkedIn community to expand your professional network, stay updated on career opportunities, and showcase your achievements:

Tell us where you’re interning or working now : Share your current internship or job experience through our quick form. Your story helps us highlight student success and connect others with opportunities.

Employers consistently tell us that A&L graduates bring something rare: the ability to think critically, communicate clearly, and adapt with creativity. These are the skills that drive innovation in every sector.
We’re proud of the impact our Arts & Letters students make in the workplace — and we’d love to hear from you.
Share your experience : Tell us about the value our students and graduates bring to your organization. Submit a quick testimonial through our Employer Testimonial/Highlights Form.
Be featured : Your story may be highlighted on our website, social media, or in publications to inspire students and strengthen employer partnerships.
Connect with us on LinkedIn : Join the [Sac State Arts & Letters LinkedIn community] to stay engaged, discover talent, and showcase your collaboration with us.

links.text: Ready to Lead, Ready to Thrive

pages.title: Humanities & Religious Studies | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Department of Humanities and Religious Studies offers an integrated approach to the study of world cultures through an interdisciplinary study of core ideas, ideals, and values across history.
headings: Department of Humanities & Religious Studies College of Arts & Letters
Spotlight on The Department Of Humanities
Connect with the Department of Humanities & Religious Studies
contentBlocks: We live and work on the tribal and cultural lands and adjacent waterways of Sacramento’s Indigenous People and acknowledge the Nisenan, the Southern Maidu, the Valley and Plains Miwok/Me-Wuk, and the Patwin Wintun peoples as the original and continuous stewards of these places.

We respect the diversity of this department, of the College, of the University, and of the wider community and seek the formation of a truly inclusive and anti-racist environment. As a result, we commit to reflection both as individuals and as a department regarding our classroom spaces, our teaching practices, our curriculum, and our communication with these communities.
The Shape of Power: Stories of Race and American Sculpture

The Department of Humanities and Religious Studies offers an integrated approach to the study of world cultures across history. In our courses, students undertake an interdisciplinary study of core ideas, ideals, and values.
Religious Studies focuses on the beliefs and practices of the world’s religions and their relationship to history and culture.

The study of the Humanities can be a profoundly personal experience, yet students acquire the concrete analytical, research, writing, and organizational skills essential to their professional careers.

The Master of Arts in Humanities at California State University, Sacramento offers a course of study that aims to challenge students to cross-cultural and disciplinary lines and encourages them to acquire a breadth and depth of knowledge that enables them to pursue a rigorous line of intellectual inquiry that is both informative and innovative. Faculty, staff, and students are committed to fostering a collaborative and vibrant community of scholars that enhances the graduate school experience and enriches both the local and intellectual community at large.
Dr. Victoria Shinbrot, Graduate Coordinator Dr. Harvey Stark, Department Chair

Connect with the Department of Theatre & Dance
contentBlocks: The Department of Theatre and Dance, strongly affirms our commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility ( DEI A), which remains fully compliant with federal and state laws, including Title VI of Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX, and California’s Proposition 209.

The faculty and staff in the Department of Theatre & Dance at Sacramento State are dedicated to educating storytellers and scholar-artists through critical engagement with the artistic process. With an emphasis on intersectional and interdisciplinary pedagogy, praxis, and performance we inspire our students to be compassionate, equitable, and productive citizen artists.
Values Artistic Diversity, Representation, and Inclusion Embracing diverse artistic expressions, narratives, and inclusive representations while celebrating diverse identities and fostering belonging
Growth, Mastery, and Guidance Providing clear guidance to help students flourish in their growth and mastery to become versatile and empowered artists
Engagement, Outreach, and Social Advocacy Engaging diverse communities through outreach programs to harness the power of art for social change
Collaboration, Innovation, and Integrity Fostering the collaborative process to celebrate creativity, while upholding artistic integrity and encouraging innovation in and out of the classroom
Community Cultivating a community rooted in mutual respect and support

pages.title: World Languages & Literatures | Sacramento State
pages.description: Our department offers academic degree paths across French, Chinese, German, Italian, Japanese, and Spanish, as well as courses in Arabic, Korean, and Punjabi.
headings: Department of World Languages & Literatures College of Arts & Letters
Welcome to the Department of World Languages & Literatures
Discover Our World Languages & Literatures Department
contentBlocks: The department office is temporarily closed.
For immediate assistance, please contact the College of Arts & Letters Dean’s Office at Mariposa Hall , Room 5000, call (916) 278-6502, or email collegeofal@csus.edu.
Please allow up to three business days for a response.

Language is a basic skill in communicating with other people, but even more importantly, language is a tool for organizing and generating ideas. Language defines and shapes how we understand the world and ourselves. Different languages give us different perceptions. By learning a new language, you not only discover a new world and make new friends, but you also discover a new you!

The Department of World Languages & Literatures at Sacramento State offers a Master of Arts in Spanish, undergraduate majors in Spanish and French, minors in Chinese, French, German, Italian, Japanese, and Spanish, and also offers language classes in Arabic, Korean, and Punjabi.

The mission of the Department of World Languages & Literatures is to provide students with the opportunity to learn about world languages, literature, and cultures to broaden cultural understanding, increase language proficiency, and enable students to communicate successfully in a pluralistic American society and abroad.

The Department of World Languages & Literatures, strongly affirms our commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility (DEIA), which remains fully compliant with federal and state laws, including Title VI of Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX, and California’s Proposition 209.
Spanish Bachelor of Arts, Minor Program, Master of Arts HEALS Program (Certificate Program in Healthcare Spanish) Certificate Program in Spanish for Criminal Justice

pages.title: Philosophy | Sacramento State
pages.description: Sacramento State offers the Bachelor of Arts degree with a major in Philosophy, as well as a Philosophy minor.
headings: Department of Philosophy College of Arts & Letters
The Center for Practical & Professional Ethics
Connect with the Department of Philosophy
contentBlocks: The subject of philosophy encompasses such fundamental issues as the scope and limits of human knowledge, the ultimate constituents of reality, the sources of value and obligation, and the nature of logic and correct reasoning.
Sacramento State offers the Bachelor of Arts degree with a major in philosophy as well as a philosophy minor.
Science, Reason, & Values (formerly Logic & Phil of Science)

The Center for Practical & Professional Ethics takes as its overriding mission to foster and enhance the place of ethics in professional and public discourse, and to foster and support ethical practice, both in the University Community and in the broader Sacramento Region. Its mission is, therefore, educative, scholarly and practical.

We offer a variety of programming throughout the academic year, including the Ethics Symposium each fall and the Nammour Symposium in the spring. All our campus events are free and open to the public. Check the Campus Events Calendar for more information, and follow CPPE on Facebook .

pages.title: Student Success in Arts & Letters | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Arts & Letters is proud to host a wealth of information and special programs designed to enhance and ensure the success of all our majors.
headings: Student Success College of Arts & Letters
About Student Success in Arts & Letters
contentBlocks: Students Achieving Greater Excellence (SAGE) is the student success center for the College of Arts & Letters. SAGE is a dedicated space for students majoring in an Arts & Letters program to receive academic support as they navigate university graduation requirements and processes. SAGE Advisors work one-on-one with students to ensure they are on the best and most efficient path toward graduation.
Calaveras Hall, Room 138 Monday -Thursday: 10:00AM - 3:00PM Friday (Zoom Only): 10:00AM - 3:00PM (Scheduled Appoinments Only)
For all advising or course planning beyond Fall 2025, please use the appointment link above or email us at sage@csus.edu , or call (916) 278-4464.

Arts & Letters Professional Seminar Series (ALPSS) is a 6-week career exploration course that teaches you how to research jobs, create your personal brand, network, and more! The course features hands-on workshops and “Made at Sac State” guest speakers who majored in degree programs in the College of Arts & Letters.

Dena Lemus​ | Sr. Graduation & Retention Coordinator​ - SAGE Coordinator​ As a first-generation college student, I learned that meeting with an Academic Advisor is an essential step in student success. During my college career, my advisors ensured that I knew the shortest and most efficient path to earning my degree. I do the same for my advisees. Students do not have to navigate their educational journey alone, we are here to help provide support and connect you to campus resources. Kya Webb​ | Graduation & Retention Coordinator - SAGE Advisor I am a proud twice-made Sac State alum with a B.S. in Health Science and an M.S. in Counseling! Not only is helping students and emphasizing the importance of wellness/stress management my passion, but my goal is to help you achieve academic success by seeing you through to a timely graduation and getting connected to various resources to aid you holistically through your college journey. May you always remember that “Mindset, habits and routines are the building blocks for success towards your educational and wellness goals.”
Follow us on https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLBkhLQzCBfPhzjubCDJiZ_0Eu1v3qgVB9

pages.title: Communication Studies | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Department of Communication Studies within the College of Arts & Letters at California State University, Sacramento
headings: Department of Communication Studies College of Arts & Letters
Connect with the Department of Communication Studies
contentBlocks: The faculty of Communication Studies, Journalism and Film is a community of professionals, educated and experienced in a diverse range of specialty areas, and united by a common body of information related to the creation, application, and understanding of messages.

The department exists to provide quality instruction that assists students in becoming intellectually aware citizens who are ethical, competent communicators. Further, the department seeks to maintain and develop the body of scholarly knowledge that unites the field.
Courses in Communication studies focus on understanding the communication process and developing skills to communicate effectively.

The Department offers a wide range of courses in interpersonal , group, and organizational communication, rhetorical theory and criticism, public relations, mass communication and research methodologies.
Commitment to Antiracism and Social Justice
The General Communication Studies major has the flexibility to select courses based in Small Group, Organizational, Interpersonal, Intercultural, Mass Communication, Rhetoric, New Media, and Film.
The Public Relations major has a theoretical base focused on the concepts of publics and how communication informs various audiences.
The Journalism major emphasizes writing, editing, and interactive media. Journalism majors can write for The State Hornet .
The Film major develops a deeper understanding of the art and practice of film and the artists whose vision have inspired generations of filmmakers.
